_meta.author: S6 reconciliation agent
_meta.inputs: findings_D01..D16 reorg arrays + findings_D15_graph + D15_orphans.json (74 orphans)
_meta.invariant: every orphan/reparent target assigned EXACTLY ONCE; no double-moves; all adoptees verified parent=null except jnj.5 (lifted from jnj)
_meta.coverage: 8 new epics adopt ~40 orphans; 33 reparents into existing epics; remainder intentionally standalone (listed in standalone_kept)

new_epics.title: Beads/devloop work-evidence rail: join external work-tracking + PR evidence to the archive
new_epics.design: Coherent theme distinct from s7ae's live-agent coordination: bind the beads task graph and PR/authoring evidence to Polylogue sessions. Substrate leg (blocking): 7fj ingests beads issue history as a source family. Join leg: 4c0 builds session<->bead cross-links + archive-rendered work loop. Contract/decision legs: lnd decides the beads-vs-assertions boundary (which state lives in bd vs user.db); lio aligns the cross-repo devloop contract on beads (Sinex parity); kph attaches the authoring session's postmortem bundle to PRs (provenance-carrying PRs). Sequence: 7fj (substrate) -> 4c0/lnd (join+boundary) -> lio/kph (cross-repo + PR provenance). Do NOT pull in h6r (belongs to analytics/uplift tower, blocks h10). 6mv's emitter task may later join this rail but is not adopted here.
new_epics.acceptance: Epic exists and owns 4c0, 7fj, lnd, lio, kph as children. 7fj is marked as the blocking substrate dep of 4c0 (dep edge 7fj->4c0 present). lnd (decision) records the beads-vs-assertions boundary with a written verdict before 4c0/lio ship against it. Each child retains its own execution-grade design/acceptance; the epic body states the sequence and non-goals (no h6r, no live-coordination scope that belongs to s7ae).

new_epics.title: Usage/cost honesty: one cross-provider token+cost ledger that survives lineage and drift
new_epics.design: Cost honesty is cross-provider correctness, NOT Hermes-specific (keep out of fs1). Spine: 5hf builds the honest cross-provider usage ledger (disjoint input/cached/output/reasoning lanes; per-model partitioning). Companion: ivsc classifies Codex state_5 token drift outside lineage replay (cross-verify archive accounting vs ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite). External residual: GH #2472 (per-model partitioning bug) is the named correctness gap 5hf's ledger must close -- track it as an external-ref from 5hf, not as a bead mirror. If a distinct lineage-token double-count companion is later filed, parent it here too.
new_epics.acceptance: Epic exists and owns 5hf + ivsc. 5hf's acceptance cites GH #2472 as the partitioning correctness bar (Ref #2472, neutral wording). ivsc's reconciliation is expressed as a devtools probe (lab probe cost-reconciliation lineage) with a pass/fail band on per-thread ratio. The epic body states that cost accounting is cross-provider and must not be nested under the Hermes bridge.

new_epics.title: Capture extension: reliability, coverage, and in-page presence
new_epics.design: Browser-extension cluster distinct from the daemon-served web workbench (bby). 3v1: MV3 extension reliability + status UX (spool health, badge, completeness signal, gemini bridge, error telemetry). 90y: in-page shadow-DOM overlay showing archive state + inline assertion/context capture on chat sites. Both are area:ingest+area:web, both scoped to the extension itself. Do NOT reparent under bby -- 3v1 is pure MV3 reliability with no daemon-reader surface. Future extension beads land here.
new_epics.acceptance: Epic exists and owns 3v1 + 90y. Epic body states the boundary: extension-side code (MV3 service worker, content scripts, shadow-DOM overlay) here; daemon-reader/web-workbench surface stays in bby; posting-channel infra (ptx/b1n) stays in bby. Each child keeps its execution-grade spec.

new_epics.title: Compaction lifecycle: pre-compaction snapshot, loss forensics, post-compaction re-grounding
new_epics.design: PROMOTION: retype existing polylogue-gjg from task->epic in place (reuse the gjg id; do not mint a new epic). gjg's design already carries four epic-sized legs that must become children: (1) PreCompact snapshot capture (persist context before Claude/Codex compaction destroys it), (2) corpus-wide compaction-loss forensics measure (quantify what auto-compaction drops), (3) opt-in re-grounding SessionStart lane armable as an ExperimentSpec, (4) the 37t handoff-triad documentation. It is already dep-linked to s7ae via d1y and references 37t; keep those cross-epic deps. Decompose into 4 children so each leg is independently ready/verifiable instead of one unschedulable mega-task.
new_epics.acceptance: gjg is typed 'epic' and its four legs exist as child beads, each PR-shaped with its own acceptance. The re-grounding lane is expressed as an ExperimentSpec (declared arms + preregistered metric) so it is measurable, not asserted. Cross-epic deps to s7ae (d1y) and the 37t handoff-triad reference are preserved. No new umbrella id is created.

new_epics.title: Configuration doctrine + DB-backed runtime preferences
new_epics.design: Clearest missing-epic signal: y4c is a doctrine spine with four dependent implementation bundles, all orphaned. Spine: y4c (great defaults, DB-backed runtime prefs in user.db, Nix module surface). Bundles: 3xx (verb-behavior + ops preferences: confirmations, judge defaults, copy-as-command), y8w (reading preferences: per-scope views, fold budgets, rows, pager), 6kh (query-scope preferences: default time window, scope filter), 1jc (learned defaults: archive proposes config as judged candidates, closing the loop to 37t's judgment gate). Sequence: y4c defines the prefs table + resolution order first; the three bundles fill lanes; 1jc layers learned proposals on top.
new_epics.acceptance: Epic exists and owns y4c (spine) + 3xx, y8w, 6kh, 1jc. y4c's design fixes the user.db prefs table shape and precedence (CLI flag > env > db-pref > default) before the bundles depend on it (dep edges y4c->3xx/y8w/6kh). 1jc is gated on 37t's judgment gate (relates-to 37t). Each bundle keeps execution-grade acceptance naming its config keys.

new_epics.title: Operational resilience: recoverable, forgettable-on-purpose, observable in prod
new_epics.design: Ops/daemon-trust cluster with no owning epic; all concern 'the archive stays trustworthy, recoverable, and observable.' 4be: restore drill -- prove backups actually restore, quarterly (untested backups are not backups). 27m: excision + secret hygiene -- the archive can forget on purpose (targeted deletion + secret scrubbing). peo: daemon death forensics -- crash trace + heartbeat sentinel + restart. s8q: make deployed Polylogue state trustworthy / captures queryable (P4, deliberately parked pending next deploy -- adopt for grouping, keep P4). Note: perf-observability beads opc/oxz were routed to 20d (interactive performance), not here (see conflicts_resolved).
new_epics.acceptance: Epic exists and owns 4be, 27m, peo, s8q. 4be produces a repeatable devtools restore-drill artifact with a pass/fail line. 27m's excision path is idempotent and leaves an audit trail. peo emits a heartbeat + crash-forensics record readable from the archive. s8q remains P4 with its parked note intact; re-triaged at next deploy. Epic body states the boundary with 20d (perf instrumentation lives in 20d; resilience/recovery lives here).

new_epics.title: Ecosystem interop + origin breadth: a two-way citizen (ingest + export lanes)
new_epics.design: Large unparented cluster whose beads literally describe being a 'two-way citizen'. Ingest breadth: t0p (capture the rest of Claude Code: todos/file-history/prompt-history/debug), uiw (origin breadth: enumerate target set + generic openai-chat-shape detector), 2qx (OriginSpec: one package per origin, dispatch order derived from descriptors), 0cg (OTel GenAI semantic-conventions ingest), 7xv (native git/repo awareness: session<->commit/branch correlation), 611 (Grok/xAI export importer), ale (external link archival). Export/interop lanes: wmj (OTel GenAI trace export), 7k7 (research-tooling export: inspect-ai/Docent), r47 (Obsidian/PKM export profile), 4g5 (expose archive as HPI module + Promnesia source). The 2qx OriginSpec/detector-registry work makes each new origin mechanical, which is why one umbrella is warranted. EXCLUDED (routed elsewhere): 7fj->beads rail (beads-history is not a chat origin); ox0->fs1 (Codex state-db mirrors the Hermes state-authoritative pattern).
new_epics.acceptance: Epic exists and owns t0p, uiw, 2qx, 0cg, 7xv, 611, ale, wmj, 7k7, r47, 4g5. 2qx (OriginSpec/descriptor registry) is marked as the enabler the other ingest beads build on (dep edges 2qx->uiw/611/0cg). Export lanes share one interop contract (a declared export-profile registry). Epic body states 7fj and ox0 are intentionally NOT here. Each child keeps its own acceptance; P4 breadth items may stay under-specified deep backlog.

new_epics.title: Substrate consolidation: kill the twins, God-modules, and duplicated primitives
new_epics.design: Internal-debt refactor cluster distinct from t46's surface-dispatch focus (t46 owns parallel *surfaces*; this owns duplicated *substrate*). Daemon nervous system: 0aj (declared write-effects chain: post-commit effects as registry entries), yp0 (daemon internal event bus: loops subscribe, polling retires). Storage-twin trap: 48h (consolidate 10 copies of _table_exists / introspection helpers), pf1 (sync/async divergence: diff the twin backends against the 10 known divergences), 1ty (fts_freshness_state declared twice: reconcile with schema policy). Dead weight: 1a9 (remove dead session-commit stubs + unused web-construct row + stale fuzz REs), dab (stop materializing run-projection cache rows; drop DDL after parity). Doctrine: c9y (package topology boundary doctrine for the 28-package tree). Note hiu (twin collapse, from D06) relates here but stays where D06 keeps it standalone; cross-link rather than move.
new_epics.acceptance: Epic exists and owns 0aj, yp0, 48h, pf1, 1ty, 1a9, dab, c9y. Consolidation beads assert the duplicate is GONE (grep shows one _table_exists, one fts_freshness declaration) not merely 'added a helper'. c9y (doctrine) lands before the boundary-sensitive moves. pf1's twin-diff is expressed as an executable check. Each child keeps its own acceptance + verify command.

reparents.why: FK-index scale bug: correct on fixtures, SCAN over ~89k v24 rows; blockers 3wb/6h7 CLOSED (ready). Populates the empty scale-hardening epic. [D06]
thread_refresh unbounded on large Codex appends (3.02s of 3.04s graph_resolve on 340MB append); same wrong-at-scale bug class; blocker 3wb CLOSED. [D06]
Full usage diagnostic D-stated on the live archive while a 30s SQL equivalent existed -- bug that only bites at real archive scale = 1xc charter. [D15]
Archive schema hygiene for evidence-cockpit read paths (unsafe joins, JSON checks) is scale-read hardening. [D15]
Decision: archive scaling doctrine (keep everything, optimize ceilings) is the decision layer over 1xc's bug layer; both cite 16.5K/42GB growth. [D15]
CONFLICT RESOLVED (mhx over 1xc): embedding daemon-drain bound; fix lives in _embed_archive_sessions_sync; overlaps mhx.6 DRAIN SCOPING. Domain epic owns it; cross-link to 1xc. [D13 over D06]

Activation layer teaches agents to use the memory loop (preamble 37t.4, protocol 37t.2, judgment gate); deliverable is CLAUDE.md context + adoption metrics = 37t. Deps d1y/pj8 (s7ae children) kept as cross-epic. Highest-priority orphan (P1). [D15]
'polylogue judge' is the human ergonomics of the 37t judgment gate; its own text cedes lifecycle semantics to 37t.1. [D15]
Decision on user.db assertion vocabulary is foundational to the claims->judgment->preamble loop 37t consumes. [D15]
Lesson-reinforcement scheduling (SRS over injection) extends the memory loop's injection stage. [D15]
'Walk the seven core agent flows end-to-end' is the empirical complement to the pj8 cookbook (an s7ae child); coordination-substrate adoption. [D15]
'Blackboard as agent comms: messages that arrive' is multi-agent coordination messaging; deps include s7ae.3. [D15]
'Express devloop state in Polylogue substrate' = write focus/handoff/proof-claims as work-event assertions = rii's charter; rii has only 2 children. [D15]
Prediction/calibration tracking (Brier, reliability buckets via 9l5.7) is outcome-grounded analytics; dep on 9l5.7 already makes it a 9l5 slice. [D15]
Experiment hosting (declared arms, preregistered metrics) depends on 9l5.7 and is analytics substrate; relates-to 37t for context experiments. [D15]
Self-tracing (daemon spans in its own archive) feeds 20d.14 latency projection; daemon/perf observability = 20d. [D15]
Perf-instrumentation doctrine (slow-query log, phase timings) complements 20d.7 EQP sweep and feeds bby.8/20d.14 acceptance. [D15]
Docs information architecture (tiered index, orphan/stale sweep) is external legibility; sibling of 3tl.9 docs coverage lint. [D15]
Generated codebase atlas (self-grokking map) feeds the README skim ladder (3tl.1). [D15]
Datasette lane ('your archive is just SQLite') targets 3tl's validator/evangelist audience and proves no-lock-in. [D15]
Docs-site verification lane (pages cache + link integrity) is legibility infra; pairs with 3tl.9. [D15]
'Docs/theming/release-proof/control-plane polish' is externally-inspectable polish = 3tl scope. [D15]
CONFLICT RESOLVED (fs1 over interop-breadth): Codex state_5.sqlite as authoritative source mirrors fs1.1's 'product-local state authoritative; snapshots fallback' pattern exactly. [D15 self-resolved]
'Harden blob hash validation + drop misleading symlink check' is blob-store integrity = 83u (Attachment & blob evidence integrity). [D15]
Consolidate the three session->HTML render paths = delete parallel dispatch, t46's charter. [D15]
Facade decomposition of the 5.2k-line God-object = the parallel-dispatch/QA-middle-layer removal t46 names. [D15]
'One status assembly' collapses three disagreeing status computations into one contract = t46 delete-parallel-dispatch. [D15]
Session changes view lands a web 'Changes' tab as evidence-cockpit content; relates-to bby.11. [D15]
Browser-capture posting channel is web-capture/posting infra the workbench surfaces; b1n depends on it. [D15]
WebUI-driven posting is UI plumbing over the ptx channel, delivered in the web workbench. [D15]
Cross-surface handoff (polylogue open + copy-as-command everywhere, one ref grammar) = jnj 'one rule per concern across CLI/config/onboarding'. [D15]
'Named bounded-dialogue layout' is a reusable read-view/render profile wired to DSL terminal stages (fnm.6); query-DSL epic owns projection/layout naming. [D15]
Layout profiles / RenderSpec presets per 4p1 extend the read-algebra substrate fnm owns; makes the read-algebra frontier auditable as one deliverable rather than a floating task. [D03]

LIFT OUT of jnj: jnj is P3 surface-hygiene; jnj.5 is a P2 area:security bug (ops reset --session/--source tombstones before confirmation, reset.py:260-277). Nesting live security work under a hygiene epic risks tier-4 deprioritization. Track standalone at true P2; keep a relates-to link to jnj for the shared mutation-contract theme. [D10]

reprioritizations.why: P3->P2. Produces the 'published finding URL' that is item (3) of the 3tl epic's OWN acceptance and is the enforcement site for cpf's provenance stanza + citation target for 3tl.3/3tl.10/3tl.8. Spine/critical-path enabler currently at deep-backlog tier while its dependents also sit at P3. [D07]

P3->P2. Resolves the priority inversion: the epic is P3 but its child bby.11 (Webui architecture v2) is P1 spine that gates s7ae/4smp web-projection work. A P3 epic reads as deep backlog while it actually carries P1-blocking architecture. (Alternative -- reparent bby.11 out to a spine epic -- rejected: no foundational web epic exists; lifting bby is lower-churn and honest.) [D11]

RETYPE decision->task (priority unchanged, P3). The decision is DECIDED (kill the TUI); remaining scope is a mechanical ~373-line deletion PR. Retyping makes it a claimable execution item instead of a phantom open decision. [D16]
RETYPE task->epic (priority unchanged, P2). See new_epics 'Compaction lifecycle': gjg carries four epic-sized legs; promote in place and decompose into children. [D05/brief]

standalone_kept.why: Proactive schema-cost audit, not a confirmed wrong-at-scale bug; D06 explicitly rejected 1xc.
Twin-collapse refactor; relates to substrate-consolidation but D06 keeps standalone; cross-link only.
P1 operational-architecture spine in its own right; not a 1xc scale bug.
Layering refactor, not a scale bug; D06 rejected 1xc.
Fix is in the Sinnix repo (devshell scope wrapper), not Polylogue code; do NOT move under 20d; link as external measurement context. [D08]
Advanced verification lanes; 2-bead 'verification depth' mini-cluster with th0 -- too small for an epic; seed a testing epic only if more test-infra beads appear. [D15]
Interactive-surface test harness; pairs with yeq; keep standalone. [D15]
Harness remote-control lane; standalone; could join s7ae/agent-orchestration later.
Advisory hooks; standalone; relates to 37t but not adopted.
Decision: one read algebra -- foundational decision feeding fnm; keep standalone (fnm/jnj already reference it).
Decision: DuckDB optional OLAP engine; standalone decision.
Decision: daemon HTTP substrate; standalone decision.
Resume-ranking bug (workflow shapes classifier never emits); standalone P2 bug.
Display-title synthesis; standalone.
Devtools surface economy; standalone.
Dependency leverage policy chore; standalone.
Chunked/streaming read-package layout; standalone (relates to fnm/read-algebra but not adopted).
Ambient theming P4; standalone.
Desktop-presence spike P4; standalone.
Native-compilation probe P4; standalone.
IssueBench P4; standalone.
Branch-local dev-loop AC verify-and-close task; standalone close-out (touches devloop but is a one-shot closure, not epic-shaped).

conflicts_resolved.justification: Embedding-specific: fix is in _embed_archive_sessions_sync (daemon-drain window checked only between sessions), and it overlaps mhx.6's DRAIN SCOPING item. The domain epic that already owns adjacent work (mhx.6) should own it; 1xc is the generic scale epic. Cross-link to 1xc. This overrides the brief's 'ma2/0ns/6wnh->1xc' shorthand for 0ns only (ma2/6wnh still -> 1xc).

conflicts_resolved.options: D15 -> 37t vs s7ae (deps d1y/pj8 are s7ae children)
conflicts_resolved.justification: Deferred to the dedicated graph agent: 3gd's deliverable is standing CLAUDE.md context + adoption metrics for the memory loop (preamble/protocol/judgment gate) = 37t. Cross-epic deps to s7ae children are kept as edges, not parenthood. Flagged as the one reparent with a live alternative.

conflicts_resolved.options: interop-breadth ingest side vs fs1 (Hermes bridge)
conflicts_resolved.justification: Graph agent self-resolved: Codex state_5.sqlite-as-authoritative mirrors fs1.1's 'product-local state authoritative; snapshots fallback' pattern exactly. Removed from the interop-breadth epic to avoid a double-move.

conflicts_resolved.options: beads/devloop rail (brief) vs interop-breadth ingest side (D15)
conflicts_resolved.justification: 7fj ingests BEADS issue history, not a chat/provider origin -- it is the substrate dep of the work-evidence rail (blocks 4c0), which the brief blessed. Removed from interop-breadth to avoid a double-move.

Both are perf instrumentation: opc (self-tracing spans) feeds 20d.14 latency projection; oxz (slow-query log, phase timings) complements 20d.7 EQP sweep. ops-resilience-epic adopts only recovery/trust beads (4be/27m/peo/s8q); the epic body states the boundary.

conflicts_resolved.options: keep under jnj (hygiene epic) vs lift to standalone P2 security bug
conflicts_resolved.justification: A live P2 area:security correctness bug must not inherit P3 hygiene-tier deprioritization; keep the shared mutation-contract theme as a relates-to link. [D10]

1xc owns confirmed-severe wrong-at-scale BUGS only. iec is a proactive audit, hiu/exb are refactors, b5l is an operational-architecture spine. Related to scale but not the bug class; only ma2/6wnh (and the D15 scale bugs xy95/xgw + doctrine fie) belong under 1xc.

conflicts_resolved.options: lift epic bby to P2 vs reparent bby.11 out to a spine epic
conflicts_resolved.justification: No foundational web/spine epic exists to receive bby.11; lifting the epic to P2 is lower-churn and honestly reflects that it carries a P1 spine child gating s7ae/4smp web work. bby.11 stays P1 under bby.
